---
title: "hausdorff95"
generated: true
description: "95th-percentile symmetric surface distance (MONAI `percentile=95`)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.metrics.surface`](../nitrix-metrics-surface.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:hausdorff95 -->
```python
hausdorff95(pred: Bool[Array, '*spatial'], target: Bool[Array, '*spatial'], *, spacing: _Spacing = None, backend: Backend = 'auto') -> Float[Array, '']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:hausdorff95 -->
95th-percentile symmetric surface distance (MONAI `percentile=95`).

Takes the maximum over the two directions of the 95th-percentile directed
surface distance:
$\max(Q_{95}(d(S_{\text{pred}} \to S_{\text{target}})),
Q_{95}(d(S_{\text{target}} \to S_{\text{pred}})))$, where
$S_{\ast}$ are the connectivity-1 surfaces and the percentile uses
linear interpolation (the `numpy` / `torch.quantile` default).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:hausdorff95 -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `pred` | `Bool` | `*spatial` | — | Binary masks (any non-zero is foreground), of the same spatial shape. |
| `target` | `Bool` | `*spatial` | — | Binary masks (any non-zero is foreground), of the same spatial shape. |
| `spacing` | `_Spacing` | — | `None` | Per-axis voxel size (scalar or one per axis); `None` uses unit spacing. The result is in these units. |
| `backend` | `Backend` | — | `'auto'` | Execution engine for the underlying [`erode`](../nitrix-morphology/erode.qmd) and Euclidean distance transform (`"auto"` / `"jax"` / `"pallas-cuda"`). |

**Returns** `Float` — shape —. Scalar HD95 in `spacing` units; $+\infty$ if either mask has no surface (is empty), matching MONAI. Non-differentiable (a reporting metric).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:hausdorff95 -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
